chapters.narration: Any destination in the forest can be reached by mixing basic directions. A linear combination a times u plus b times v is a recipe — different amounts of each base vector create a new result. Think of it like mixing paint or scaling a recipe: two parts east plus three parts north lands you somewhere neither direction alone could reach.

The span of u and v is every vector you can build from them — all possible recipes. With two non-parallel base vectors in the plane, the span fills the entire two-dimensional space. Every point on the map becomes reachable.
